steps.title: Attach the storage phrase
steps.content: The phrase after the blank, “the former in a repository…” and “the latter in a repository…,” gives extra information about where the two specimens are preserved. It has no finite verb, so it cannot stand alone as a sentence.

steps.title: Avoid fragments and false joins
steps.content: A creates a sentence fragment after the period. C uses a semicolon even though what follows is not an independent clause. D adds and, making the phrase seem like a coordinated clause rather than an explanatory appositive. A comma attaches the phrase correctly. The answer is B.
